factors.summary: Overall demand is decelerating, particularly in the energy sector due to the impact of COVID and a shift toward energy transition.

evidence.text: Our business has been adversely affected by the impacts of COVID and the steep decline in oil prices that occurred in early 2020. These events created significant uncertainty and economic volatility and disruption, which have impacted and may continue to impact our business.

factors.summary: The company is not successfully raising prices; cost increases are offsetting pricing power.

factors.summary: Free cash flow has decreased due to lower operating cash flow and deteriorating generation from project cancellations and increased costs.

evidence.text: Increase (decrease) in cash and cash equivalents (73,266) 96,583

The effective tax rate on earnings (loss) from Cont Ops was 38.4% for the 2021 Quarter and 97.8% for the 2021 Period compared to 52.6% and (5.3)% for the corresponding periods of 2020. For all periods, the effective tax rate was unfavorably impacted by increases in the valuation allowances against foreign tax credit carryforwards and certain foreign net operating loss carryforwards.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Decreasing free cash flow due to lower operating cash flow and project cancellations, indicating financial strain.

factors.summary: The company is implementing cost reduction programs to address increased G&A expenses and tax payments, which have negatively impacted operating cash flow. However, the full impact of these measures has not yet been realized.

evidence.text: Our operating cash flow for the 2021 Period was negatively impacted by increased funding of COVID costs on our projects, higher cash payments of G&A (including the timing and extent of employee bonuses) and increased tax payments, most of which occurred in the first quarter of 2021.

We announced in January 2021 that we had begun an undertaking to substantially reduce our overhead costs. Although we have not satisfied the requirements to recognize charges for any restructurings for the 2021 undertaking, we are likely to recognize expense in future quarters for these efforts.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction programs aim to address increased expenses but have not yet shown positive results.

factors.summary: The company's approach to shareholder returns is mixed. While they have a stock repurchase program available, with over 10 million shares still purchasable, there is no immediate intent to repurchase shares. Additionally, the company is focusing on reducing overhead costs, which may lead to reduced shareholder returns in the future.

evidence.text: As of September 30, 2021, over 10 million shares could still be purchased under the existing stock repurchase program, although we don't have any immediate intent to begin such repurchases.

We announced in January 2021 that we had begun an undertaking to substantially reduce our overhead costs. Although we have not satisfied the requirements to recognize charges for any restructurings for the 2021 undertaking, we are likely to recognize expense in future quarters for these efforts.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Mixed approach with no immediate intent to repurchase shares and focus on reducing costs, potentially harming shareholder returns.

factors.summary: The company is managing its debt by repurchasing notes and planning to extend its credit facility, but this activity reflects rising interest costs and refinancing concerns, indicating a mixed approach to debt leverage.

evidence.text: In September 2021, we completed a tender offer in which we repurchased $375 million (face value) of 2023 Notes and $108 million (face value) of 2024 Notes, excluding accrued interest. Additionally, we redeemed $26 million of outstanding 2023 and 2024 Notes in open market transactions during the 2021 Period. We used the proceeds from the issuance of preferred stock to redeem the 2023 and 2024 Notes. We recognized $20 million in losses related to these redemptions which is included in interest expense.

The loss on debt repurchases includes costs to redeem $509 million of 2023 and 2024 Notes through a tender offer completed in September 2021 as well as through open market transactions following the issuance of the CPS in the second quarter of 2021.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Debt management activities reflect rising interest costs and refinancing concerns, indicating financial stress.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are under pressure and compressing due to higher expenses, project execution challenges, cost increases, and project cancellations.

evidence.text: Our operating cash flow for the 2021 Period was negatively impacted by increased funding of COVID costs on our projects, higher cash payments of G&A (including the timing and extent of employee bonuses) and increased tax payments, most of which occurred in the first quarter of 2021.
The change in segment profit margin reflects these same factors.

sentiment.label: very_negative

factors.summary: Organic growth is facing challenges due to decreased operating cash flow from increased COVID-related expenses, higher G&A payments, and elevated tax payments. Additionally, revenue growth is decelerating as a result of project cancellations and reduced demand, particularly from energy clients who have not increased their capital expenditures despite rebounding oil prices.

evidence.text: Our operating cash flow for the 2021 Period was negatively impacted by increased funding of COVID costs on our projects, higher cash payments of G&A (including the timing and extent of employee bonuses) and increased tax payments, most of which occurred in the first quarter of 2021.

Although oil prices have rebounded in 2021, we have not yet seen our energy clients respond with elevated capital expenditures for our services. Our estimates reflect our best assessment of project results inclusive of COVID effects, which have been dynamic as our projects have seen changes in prevailing regulations as COVID cases crested and fell.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is challenged by decreased cash flow, project cancellations, and reduced demand, particularly from energy clients.
